result:
**Proposed Goal: Add an Abelian Sandpile simulation mode (Shift+J)**

The project has 17 simulation modes covering fluid dynamics, particle systems, cellular automata, and ecology — but nothing demonstrating **self-organized criticality**. The Abelian Sandpile model fills that gap perfectly: drop "sand grains" onto a grid, and when any cell reaches 4 grains it topples, distributing one grain to each neighbor. This creates cascading avalanches of all sizes and produces stunning fractal patterns — all from a dead-simple rule. It would include:

- Configurable toppling threshold
- A "drop mode" (continuous center drop vs. random drops vs. click-to-drop)
- Color-coded cells by grain count (0–3+ grains)
- Avalanche size tracking with a live power-law distribution sparkline
- Preset: the "identity" sandpile (the beautiful fractal that emerges when you start from max and relax)

This is strategically the best next addition because it introduces a fundamentally new concept (criticality / power-law dynamics) not covered by any existing mode, while being visually spectacular and computationally elegant.
